services.name: pub.string:base64Decode
services.description: WmPublic. Decodes a Base-64 encoded string into a sequence of bytes.

input_parameters.name: string
input_parameters.description: String A Base64-encoded String to decode into bytes.

input_parameters.name: encoding
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Specifies the encoding method for the string value. That is, describes how the input Base64-encoded String is constructed. Set to: ASCII. This is the default. UTF-8 If the Base64-encoded String in string was generated by the pub.string:base64Encode service, the value of the encoding input parameter for pub.string:base64Decode must match the value of the encoding parameter used in the pub.string:base64Encode service invocation. If the Base64-encoded String in string was obtained from somewhre besides the pub.string:base64Encode service, set the encoding parameter to ASCII.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: byte[ ] The sequence of bytes decoded from the Base64-encoded String.

services.name: pub.string:base64Encode
services.description: WmPublic. Converts a sequence of bytes into a Base64-encoded String.

input_parameters.name: bytes
input_parameters.description: byte[ ] Sequence of bytes to encode into a Base64-encoded String. The input byte[] can be any Java supported charset-encoded byte[].

input_parameters.name: useNewLine
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Flag indicating whether to retain or remove the line breaks. Set to: true to retain the line breaks. This is the default. false to remove the line breaks.

input_parameters.name: encoding
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Specifies the encoding method to be used when encoding bytes. Set to: ASCII. This is the default. UTF-8

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Base64-encoded String encoded from the sequence of bytes.

usage_notes: By default, the pub.string:base64Encode service inserts line breaks after 76 characters of data, which is not the canonical lexical form expected by implementations such as MTOM. You can use the useNewLine parameter to remove the line breaks. For more information about MTOM implementations, refer to Web Services Developer’s Guide .

services.name: pub.string:bytesToString
services.description: WmPublic. Converts a sequence of bytes to a String.

input_parameters.name: bytes
input_parameters.description: byte[ ] Sequence of bytes to convert to a String.

input_parameters.name: encoding
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Name of a registered, IANA character set (for example, ISO-8859-1). If you specify an unsupported encoding, the system throws an exception. To use the default encoding, set encoding to autoDetect.

input_parameters.name: ignoreBOMChars
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Flag indicating whether or not Integration Server removes the byte order mark (BOM) characters in the input sequence of bytes before converting the byte array to string. Set to: true to remove the byte order mark (BOM) characters before converting the input sequence of bytes to string, if the byte array contains BOM characters. false to include the byte order mark (BOM) characters while converting the input sequence of bytes to string. The default is false.

output_parameters.name: string
output_parameters.description: String String representation of the contents of bytes.

services.name: pub.string:compareStrings
services.description: WmPublic. Performs a case-sensitive comparison of two strings, and indicates whether the strings are identical.

input_parameters.name: inString1
input_parameters.description: String Optional. String to compare against inString2. This input variable can be null.

input_parameters.name: inString2
input_parameters.description: String Optional. String to compare against inString1. This input variable can be null.

output_parameters.name: isEqual
output_parameters.description: String Indicates whether or not inString1 and inString2 are identical. true indicates that inString1 and inString2 are identical. false indicates that inString1 and inString2 are not identical. Note: If both inString1 and inString2 are null, the service considers the strings to be identical and returns true.

services.name: pub.string:concat
services.description: WmPublic. Concatenates two strings.

input_parameters.name: inString1
input_parameters.description: String String to which you want to concatenate another string.

input_parameters.name: inString2
input_parameters.description: String String to concatenate to inString1.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Result of concatenating inString1 with inString2 (inString1 + inString2).

services.name: pub.string:HTMLDecode
services.description: WmPublic. Replaces HTML character entities with native characters.

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String An HTML-encoded String.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Result from decoding the contents of inString. Any HTML character entities that existed in inString will appear as native characters in value.

services.name: pub.string:HTMLEncode
services.description: WmPublic. Replaces HTML-sensitive characters with equivalent HTML character entities.

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String The character you want to encode in HTML.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Result from encoding the contents of inString. Any HTML-sensitive characters that existed in inString (for example, > or &) will appear as the equivalent HTML character entities in value.

services.name: pub.string:indexOf
services.description: WmPublic. Returns the index of the first occurrence of a sequence of characters in a string.

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String String in which you want to locate a sequence of characters.

input_parameters.name: subString
input_parameters.description: String Sequence of characters to locate.

input_parameters.name: fromIndex
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Index of inString from which to start the search. If no value is specified, this parameter contains 0 to indicate the beginning of the string.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Index of the first occurrence of subString in inString. If no occurrence is found, this parameter contains -1.

services.name: pub.string:isAlphanumeric
services.description: WmPublic. Determines whether a string consists entirely of alphanumeric characters (in the ranges A–Z, a–z, or 0–9).

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String Optional. String to be checked for alphanumeric characters.

output_parameters.name: isAlphanumeric
output_parameters.description: String Indicates whether or not all the characters in inString are alphanumeric. true indicates that all the characters in inString are alphanumeric. false indicates that not all the characters in inString are alphanumeric. The service returns false if inString is not specified.

services.name: pub.string:isDate
services.description: WmPublic. Determines whether a string follows a specified date pattern.

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String Optional. String to be checked for adherence to the specified date pattern.

input_parameters.name: pattern
input_parameters.description: String Date format for specifying the inString parameter (for example, yyyyMMdd HH:mm:ss.SSS). For more information about the pattern strings that can be specified for the date, see Pattern String Symbols.

output_parameters.name: isDate
output_parameters.description: String Indicates whether or not inString follows the specified date pattern. true indicates that inString follows the specified date pattern. false indicates that inString does not follow the specified date pattern. The service returns false if inString is not specified.

usage_notes: The service returns an error if both inString and pattern are not specified.

You can specify any random string (for example, 111212) as both inString and pattern. The service returns true if the same user-defined string is specified as both inString and pattern. This is because the java.text.SimpleDateFormat class parses the user-defined input string and pattern to a valid date when the particular input values are identical.

You must ensure that the date you provide as inString has the same format as pattern. Because the java.text.SimpleDateFormat class allows you to specify any user-defined input string as a date format, the pub.string:isDate service might return true even if the inString format does not match the specified pattern.

services.name: pub.string:isNullEmptyOrWhitespace
services.description: WmPublic. Determines if a string is null, empty, or only whitespace.

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String Optional. String to be checked.

input_parameters.name: ifPresent
input_parameters.description: Boolean Optional. If the value is set to true, the service checks whether inString is present or not, and only then proceeds with validation of the input. If the value is set to false, the service throws an exception when inString is absent. Else, the service proceeds to validate the input.

output_parameters.name: isNullEmptyOrWhitespace
output_parameters.description: String Indicates whether inString is null, empty, or only whitespace. true indicates that inString has a null value, is empty, or is only whitespace. false indicates that inString is not null, not empty, or is not only whitespace.

usage_notes: pub.string:isNullEmptyOrWhitespace replaces pub.string:isNullOrBlank which is deprecated.

services.name: pub.string:isNullOrBlank
services.description: WmPublic. Deprecated - Use pub.string:isNullEmptyOrWhitespace.

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String Optional. String to be checked.

input_parameters.name: ifPresent
input_parameters.description: Boolean Optional. If the value is set to true, the service checks whether inString is present or not, and only then proceeds with validation of the input. If the value is set to false, the service throws an exception when inString is absent. Else, the service proceeds to validate the input.

output_parameters.name: isNullorBlank
output_parameters.description: String Indicates whether or not inString has a null or empty value. true indicates that inString has either a null value or an empty value. false indicates that inString contains a value that is not null or not empty.

services.name: pub.string:isNumber
services.description: WmPublic. Determines whether the contents of a string can be converted to a float value.

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String Optional. String to be checked for conversion to float.

output_parameters.name: isNumber
output_parameters.description: String Indicates whether or not inString can be converted to a float value. true indicates that inString can be converted to a float value. false indicates that inString cannot be converted to a float value. The service returns false if inString is not specified.

services.name: pub.string:length
services.description: WmPublic. Returns the length of a string.

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String String whose length you want to discover.

input_parameters.name: encoding
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Specifies the IANA character set to use when determining the string length. To use the default encoding of the JVM, either set encoding to autodetect or do not specify a value. The pub.string:length service ends with an exception if JVM does not recognize the specified encoding. Note: The encoding input is available after applying a fix that includes PIE-98798 (IS_10.15_Core_Fix21).

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String The number of characters in inString.

services.name: pub.string:lookupDictionary
services.description: WmPublic. Looks up a given key in a hash table and returns the string to which that key is mapped.

input_parameters.name: hashtable
input_parameters.description: java.util.Hashtable Hash table that uses String objects for keys and values.

input_parameters.name: key
input_parameters.description: String Key in hashtable whose value you want to retrieve. Note: The key is case sensitive.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Value of the string to which key is mapped. If the requested key in hashtable is null or if key is not mapped to any value in hashtable, the service returns null.

services.name: pub.string:lookupTable
services.description: WmPublic. Locates a key in a String Table and returns the string to which that key is mapped.

input_parameters.name: lookupTable
input_parameters.description: String [ ] [ ] A multi-row, multi-column string table in which to search.

input_parameters.name: keyColumnIndex
input_parameters.description: String Index of the "key" column. Default is 0.

input_parameters.name: valueColumnIndex
input_parameters.description: String Index of the "value" column. Default is 1.

input_parameters.name: key
input_parameters.description: String Key to locate. Note: The key is case sensitive.

input_parameters.name: ignoreCase
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Flag indicating whether to perform a case-sensitive or case-insensitive search. Set to: true to perform a case-insensitive search. false to perform a case-sensitive search. This is the default.

input_parameters.name: useRegex
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Flag indicating whether the values in the table are to be interpreted as regular expressions. Note: The regular expressions in the table should not include slashes. For example, use hello.*, not /hello.*/. Set to: true to interpret the key column values in the table as regular expressions. false to interpret the key column values in the table as literal values (that is, not regular expressions). This is the default.

input_parameters.name: regexTimeout
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. Specifies the maximum execution time in milliseconds for regex matching. Integration Server supports the Perl5 (Apache ORO) and Java Regex engines for regex matching. When regexTimeout is set, Integration Server uses Java Regex regardless of the configured regex engine. If not set, the configured regex engine (Perl5 or Java Regex) is used. By default, the parameter value is blank.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String First value in the "value" column whose key matches key. If no match is found, this parameter is null.

usage_notes: The pub.string:lookupTable service can treat the key column values of the table as regular expressions when useRegex is set to true. By default, Integration Server uses the Perl 5 pattern matching provided by the Jakarta ORO libraries. If you want Integration Server to use Java regular expression pattern matching provided in the java.util.regex.Pattern class, set watt.server.usejavaregex to true. After enabling Java regular expression pattern matching, if execution of pub.string:lookupTable service results in the java.util.regex.PatternSyntaxException, you may need to change the syntax of the supplied pattern in delim. As an example, if your regular expression has a value of “(\\|\*|\?|{)”, it needs to be changed with “(\\|\*|\?|\{)” i.e., characters like { or }, need to be escaped with \.

services.name: pub.string:makeString
services.description: WmPublic. Builds a single string by concatenating the elements of a String List.

input_parameters.name: elementList
input_parameters.description: String List Strings to concatenate.

input_parameters.name: separator
input_parameters.description: String String to insert between each non-null element in elementList.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Result from concatenating the strings in elementList. Strings are separated by the characters specified in separator.

services.name: pub.string:messageFormat
services.description: WmPublic. Formats an array of strings into a given message pattern.

input_parameters.name: pattern
input_parameters.description: String Message that includes "placeholders" where elements from argumentList are to be inserted. The message can contain any sequence of characters. Use the {n} placeholder to insert elements from argumentList, where n is the index of the element that you want to insert. For example, the following pattern string inserts elements 0 and 1 into the message: Test results: {0} items passed, {1} items failed. Note: Do not use any characters except digits for n.

input_parameters.name: argumentList
input_parameters.description: String List Optional. List of strings to use to populate pattern. If argumentList is not supplied, the service will not replace placeholders in pattern with actual values.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Result from substituting argumentList into pattern. If pattern is empty or null, this parameter is null.

input_parameters.name: num
input_parameters.description: String The number to format.

input_parameters.name: pattern
input_parameters.description: String A pattern string that describes the way in which num is to be formatted:

input_parameters.name: num
input_parameters.description: That num will be multiplied by 100 and shown as a percentage.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String num formatted according to pattern. If pattern is an empty (not null) string, the default pattern of comma separators is used and the number of digits after the decimal point remains unchanged.

services.name: pub.string:objectToString
services.description: WmPublic. Converts an object to string representation using the Java toString() method of the object.

input_parameters.name: object
input_parameters.description: Object The object to be converted to string representation.

output_parameters.name: string
output_parameters.description: String String representation of the input object converted using the Java toString() method of the object.

services.name: pub.string:padLeft
services.description: WmPublic. Pads a string to a specified length by adding pad characters to the beginning of the string.

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String String that you want to pad.

input_parameters.name: padString
input_parameters.description: String Characters to use to pad inString.

input_parameters.name: length
input_parameters.description: String Total length of the resulting string, including pad characters.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Contents of inString preceded by as many pad characters as needed so that the total length of the string equals length.

usage_notes: If padString is longer than one character and does not fit exactly into the resulting string, the beginning of padString is aligned with the beginning of the resulting string. For example, suppose inString equals shipped and padString equals x9y.
If inString is longer than length characters, only the last length characters from inString are returned. For example, if inString equals acct1234 and length equals 4, value will contain 1234.

services.name: pub.string:padRight
services.description: WmPublic. Pads a string to a specified length by adding pad characters to the end of the string.

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String String that you want to pad.

input_parameters.name: padString
input_parameters.description: String Characters to use to pad inString.

input_parameters.name: length
input_parameters.description: String Total length of the resulting string, including pad characters.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Contents of inString followed by as many pad characters as needed so that the total length of the string equals length.

usage_notes: If padString is longer than one character and does not fit exactly into the resulting string, the end of padString is aligned with the end of the resulting string. For example, suppose inString equals shipped and padString equals x9y.
If inString is longer than length characters, only the first length characters from inString are returned. For example, if inString equals 1234acct and length equals 4, value will contain 1234.

services.name: pub.string:replace
services.description: WmPublic. Replaces all occurrences of a specified substring with a substitute string.

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String String containing the substring to replace.

input_parameters.name: searchString
input_parameters.description: String Substring to replace within inString.

input_parameters.name: replaceString
input_parameters.description: String Character sequence that will replace searchString. If this parameter is null or empty, the service removes all occurrences of searchString from inString.

input_parameters.name: useRegex
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Flag indicating whether searchString is a regular expression. When regular expressions are used to specify a search string, replaceString may also contain interpolation variables (for example, "$1") that match parenthetical subexpressions in searchString. Set to: true to indicate that searchString is a regular expression. false to indicate that searchString is not a regular expression. This is the default.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Contents of inString with replacements made.

usage_notes: The pub.string:replace service accepts a regular expression as an input. By default, Integration Server uses the Perl 5 pattern matching provided by the Jakarta ORO libraries. If you want Integration Server to use Java regular expression pattern matching provided in the java.util.regex.Pattern class, set watt.server.usejavaregex to true. After enabling Java regular expression pattern matching, if execution of pub.string:replace service results in the java.util.regex.PatternSyntaxException, you may need to change the syntax of the supplied pattern in searchString. As an example, if your regular expression has a value of “(\\|\*|\?|{)”, it needs to be changed with “(\\|\*|\?|\{)” i.e., characters like { or }, need to be escaped with \.

services.name: pub.string:stringToBytes
services.description: WmPublic. Converts a string to a byte array.

input_parameters.name: string
input_parameters.description: String String to convert to a byte[ ].

input_parameters.name: encoding
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Name of a registered, IANA character set that specifies the encoding to use when converting the String to an array of bytes (for example: ISO-8859-1). To use the default encoding, set this value to autoDetect. If you specify an unsupported encoding, an exception will be thrown.

output_parameters.name: bytes
output_parameters.description: byte[ ] Contents of string represented as a byte[ ].

services.name: pub.string:substitutePipelineVariables
services.description: WmPublic. Replaces a pipeline variable with its corresponding value.

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String Optional. String containing the pipeline variable to replace. Specify the name of the pipeline variable between the % symbols (for example, %phone% or %array_name[n]%).

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Contents of inString with the pipeline variable replaced.

usage_notes: The service returns an error if inString is not specified.
If inString does not contain a pipeline variable (a variable or an array element) between the % symbols, the service does not perform any variable substitution from the pipeline.

If you want to include the % symbol in the output, you can specify it as \% in inString. To specify the value of the pipeline variable as a percentage in the output, append \% after the variable name in inString. For example, suppose a pipeline variable revenueIncreasePercent has a value of 100.
The service cannot be used for substitution of global variables.

services.name: pub.string:substring
services.description: WmPublic. Returns a substring of a given string.

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String String from which to extract a substring.

input_parameters.name: beginIndex
input_parameters.description: String Beginning index of the substring to extract (inclusive).

input_parameters.name: endIndex
input_parameters.description: String Ending index of the substring to extract (exclusive). If this parameter is null or empty, the substring will extend to the end of inString.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Substring from beginIndex and extending to the character at endIndex - 1.

services.name: pub.string:tokenize
services.description: WmPublic. Tokenizes a string using specified delimiter characters and generates a String List from the resulting tokens.

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String String you want to tokenize (that is, break into delimited chunks).

input_parameters.name: delim
input_parameters.description: String Delimiter characters. If null or empty, the service uses the default delimiters \t\n\r, where t, n, and r represent the white space characters tab, new line, and carriage return).

input_parameters.name: useRegex
input_parameters.description: Boolean Optional If the value is set to true, Integration Server supports recognizing the delimiter character set for the delim parameter as a regular expression. If the value is set to false, Integration Server considers the delimiter character set for the delim parameter as an individual character. This is the default.

output_parameters.name: valueList
output_parameters.description: String List Strings containing the tokens extracted from inString.

services.name: pub.string:toLower
services.description: WmPublic. Converts all characters in a given string to lowercase.

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String String to convert.

input_parameters.name: language
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Lowercase, two-letter ISO-639 code. If this parameter is null, the system default is used.

input_parameters.name: country
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Uppercase, two-letter ISO-3166 code. If this parameter is null, the system default is used.

input_parameters.name: variant
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Vendor and browser-specific code. If null, this parameter is ignored.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Contents of inString, with all uppercase characters converted to lowercase.

services.name: pub.string:toUpper
services.description: WmPublic. Converts all characters in a given string to uppercase.

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String String to convert.

input_parameters.name: language
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Lowercase, two-letter ISO-639 code. If this parameter is null, the system default is used.

input_parameters.name: country
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Uppercase, two-letter ISO-3166 code. If this parameter is null, the system default is used.

input_parameters.name: variant
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Vendor and browser-specific code. If null, this parameter is ignored.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Contents of inString, with all lowercase characters converted to uppercase.

services.name: pub.string:trim
services.description: WmPublic. Trims leading and trailing white space from a given string.

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String String to trim.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Contents of inString with white space trimmed from both ends.

services.name: pub.string:URLDecode
services.description: WmPublic. Decodes a URL-encoded string.

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String URL-encoded string to decode.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Result from decoding inString. If inString contained plus (+) signs, they will appear in value as spaces. If inString contained %hex encoded characters, they will appear in value as the appropriate native character.

services.name: pub.string:URLEncode
services.description: WmPublic. URL-encodes a string.

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String String to URL-encode.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Result from URL-encoding inString. If inString contained non-alphanumeric characters (except -_.*@), they will appear in value as their URL-encoded equivalents (% followed by a two-digit hex code). If inString contained spaces, they will appear in value as plus (+) signs.
